steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content: The sentence reads: "SN 1940B was a [BLANK] explosion of a massive star, which blasts large amounts... into space—that was first detected by American astronomer Josef Johnson." The closing em dash before "that was first detected" means a parenthetical is being closed. To correctly bracket the insertion "explosion of a massive star, which blasts... into space," the blank must open with an em dash.

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content:
Identify the structure on each side: left side is "SN 1940B was a [BLANK]"; the parenthetical "explosion of a massive star, which blasts... into space" is closed by an em dash. The blank must open that parenthetical with a matching em dash.
  • "supernova, the": "SN 1940B was a supernova, the explosion of a massive star...—that was first detected" — a comma opens the appositive but the closing punctuation is an em dash; mismatched brackets. ✗
  • "supernova. The": "SN 1940B was a supernova. The explosion of a massive star...—that was first detected" — period creates two sentences; the second begins with no subject for the trailing "that was first detected." ✗
  • "supernova—the": "SN 1940B was a supernova—the explosion of a massive star, which blasts... into space—that was first detected" — em dash opens the appositive, matching the closing em dash; the parenthetical is correctly bracketed. ✓
  • "supernova: the": "SN 1940B was a supernova: the explosion of a massive star...—that was first detected" — colon opens but an em dash closes; mismatched brackets. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
